description: Fetal assessment, neonatal physiology, and perinatal diagnostics.

facts.context: The lecithin/sphingomyelin (L/S) ratio indicates fetal lung maturity when:
facts.truth: L/S ratio >= 2.0 indicates maturity; < 1.5 indicates immaturity
distractors: L/S ratio >= 3.5 indicates maturity; < 2.5 indicates immaturity
L/S ratio >= 1.0 indicates maturity; < 0.5 indicates immaturity
L/S ratio >= 2.0 indicates immaturity; < 1.5 indicates maturity

facts.context: The Bishop score guides labor induction decisions:
facts.truth: score >= 8 is favorable for induction; < 6 is unfavorable
distractors: score >= 12 is favorable for induction; < 10 is unfavorable
score >= 5 is favorable for induction; < 2 is unfavorable
score >= 8 is unfavorable for induction; < 6 is favorable

facts.context: APGAR scores at 1 minute are interpreted as:
distractors: 9-10 normal, 5-8 moderate depression, 0-4 severe depression
5-10 normal, 2-4 moderate depression, 0-1 severe depression
8-10 normal, 6-7 moderate depression, 0-5 severe depression

facts.context: The sFlt-1/PlGF ratio in preeclampsia screening:
facts.truth: ratio < 38 rules out preeclampsia developing within one week
distractors: ratio < 100 rules out preeclampsia developing within one month
ratio > 38 rules out preeclampsia developing within one week
ratio < 10 rules out preeclampsia developing within six months

facts.context: Normal fetal heart rate baseline range is defined as:
facts.truth: 110-160 beats per minute on continuous monitoring
distractors: 80-120 beats per minute on continuous monitoring
140-200 beats per minute on continuous monitoring
60-100 beats per minute on continuous monitoring

facts.context: Amniotic fluid index (AFI) defines volume abnormalities as:
facts.truth: oligohydramnios at AFI < 5 cm, polyhydramnios at AFI > 25 cm
distractors: oligohydramnios at AFI < 10 cm, polyhydramnios at AFI > 30 cm
oligohydramnios at AFI < 2 cm, polyhydramnios at AFI > 15 cm
oligohydramnios at AFI < 8 cm, polyhydramnios at AFI > 20 cm

facts.context: Neonatal phototherapy threshold for term infants at 48 hours is:
facts.truth: total serum bilirubin approximately 15 mg/dL per AAP guidelines
distractors: total serum bilirubin approximately 25 mg/dL per AAP guidelines
total serum bilirubin approximately 8 mg/dL per AAP guidelines
total serum bilirubin approximately 20 mg/dL per AAP guidelines

facts.truth: production begins around 24 weeks, adequate levels by 35 weeks gestation
distractors: production begins around 16 weeks, adequate levels by 28 weeks gestation
production begins around 30 weeks, adequate levels by 40 weeks gestation
production begins around 20 weeks, adequate levels by 24 weeks gestation

facts.context: The ductus arteriosus in term newborns:
facts.truth: functionally closes within 24-48 hours after birth from rising PaO2
distractors: remains patent for 7-10 days after birth before closing gradually
closes immediately at the moment of umbilical cord clamping
functionally closes within 1-2 weeks from falling prostaglandin

facts.context: Foramen ovale closure after birth occurs because:
facts.truth: left atrial pressure rises above right atrial pressure, pressing septum closed
distractors: right atrial pressure rises above left atrial pressure after first breath
prostaglandin E2 levels drop suddenly, triggering muscular contraction
umbilical vein closure directly seals the foramen via pressure equalization

facts.context: Exchange transfusion threshold for term neonates is:
facts.truth: total serum bilirubin approximately 25 mg/dL per AAP guidelines
distractors: total serum bilirubin approximately 15 mg/dL per AAP guidelines
total serum bilirubin approximately 35 mg/dL per AAP guidelines
total serum bilirubin approximately 20 mg/dL per AAP guidelines

facts.truth: BP >= 140/90 plus proteinuria > 300 mg/24h after 20 weeks gestation
distractors: BP >= 160/110 plus proteinuria > 500 mg/24h after 34 weeks gestation
BP >= 120/80 plus proteinuria > 150 mg/24h after 12 weeks gestation
BP >= 140/90 without proteinuria required at any gestational age
